summary: Iteration 12 — Regression test for the blank-home-page bug caused by cross-site-iframe cookie blocking. Verified the two-track auth fix: (a) HttpOnly cookie SameSite=None;Secure, and (b) in-memory JWT + axios Bearer interceptor as fallback when 3rd-party cookies are blocked. THE KEY TEST — clearing cookies mid-session and using SPA nav — PASSED: /app/orders loaded 42 rows and /app/notifications loaded 39 cards purely via the in-memory Bearer token. Export via axios+Bearer+blob also works.
critical_code_review_comments: lib/api.js: solid design — in-memory _authToken via module var (not localStorage) is XSS-safer, and the request interceptor unconditionally attaches Bearer when present. Backend precedence (Bearer > cookie) is correct for the iframe scenario.
AuthContext: setAuthToken(data.access_token) is correctly called on pilot-login, login, and register; cleared on logout. No stale-token risk.

Minor: on full page reload the in-memory token is lost — pilot mode masks this via auto-login, but a production user in a 3rd-party-cookie-blocked iframe would need to log in on every reload. Acceptable for pilot; a documented limitation for production.
server.py still >2000 lines — modularisation still recommended (carried over from iter 10/11).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 12 confirmed the iframe/cookie-blocked fix. Playwright evidence: (1) Fresh /app load → 'Centro di Comando' rendered (body 1514 chars) with 4 cookies including ordia_token. (2) context.clear_cookies() → 0 cookies remaining. (3) Click [data-testid=nav-orders] → /app/orders rendered 42 tbody rows (body 2003 chars). (4) Click [data-testid=nav-notifications] → /app/notifications rendered 39 notification-* testid cards (body 6879 chars). (5) Click nav-dashboard → 'Centro di Comando' rendered again. (6) OrderReview: opened order e2a5537d, clicked [data-testid=export-menu-button] → [data-testid=export-csv-button] → downloaded 'ordine-e2a5537d.csv' successfully. Network: exactly 2 x 401 on /api/auth/me (the expected pre-pilot-login pair). Console errors: only the 2 expected 401 log lines. Page errors (uncaught exceptions): 0. The Bearer interceptor path is proven to keep the app fully functional with zero cookies.
rca of the issue: Original bug: cross-site-iframe → SameSite=Lax cookie dropped on every request → all authenticated API calls 401 → home rendered empty. Fix verified in this iteration: (a) cookie now SameSite=None;Secure so it survives cross-site when available; (b) even when cookies are blocked entirely, the in-memory _authToken in lib/api.js is attached as Authorization: Bearer via axios request interceptor, and the backend's get_current_user gives Bearer precedence over the cookie. The cookie-cleared reproduction (which precisely simulates the iframe 3rd-party-cookie-blocked scenario) shows Orders and Notifications loading full data via SPA nav — proving the Bearer path is the working path and the blank-home regression is resolved.
